title: Report Channel document
description: A machine-readable self-description of where and how to report issues about a tool or service. Generalizes security.txt (RFC 9116) from a fixed security contact to any report channel. See docs/REPORT-CHANNEL.md. This schema describes the discovery DOCUMENT (the payload served at /.well-known/report-channel.json, emitted by `<tool> report-target --json`, or embedded in a manifest); each entry in `channels` is a report_channel descriptor.

$schema.description: Optional pointer to this schema for editor tooling; ignored by consumers.

version.description: Descriptor format version. Bumped only on a breaking change to the shape.

channels.description: One or more report channels, most-preferred first. Consumers pick the first channel they can drive.

^x-.description: Reserved for forward-compatible, vendor-namespaced extensions (see ADR).

report_channel.title: report_channel descriptor
report_channel.description: One place issues can be reported. Minimal by design: `kind` + `target` are load-bearing; `verb` and `labels` are optional hints.

kind.description: Channel type. Determines how `target` is interpreted: beads-rig -> a rig identity (`<provider>/<org>/<repo>` triplet or rig id) filed via `ws report`; github-issues -> an `owner/repo` slug; url -> an absolute https intake endpoint/form; email -> an addressee.

target.description: The address of the channel, interpreted per `kind`.

verb.description: Optional how-to hint: a copy-pasteable command or one-line instruction for filing here (e.g. `ws report <rig> "<title>"`). Advisory only — never required to use the channel.

labels.description: Optional labels/tags a filer SHOULD apply to reports on this channel.

^x-.description: Reserved for forward-compatible, vendor-namespaced extensions (see ADR).
